welcome.headline: Welcome, craftersaud
welcome.subheadline: You're the owner of this workspace. Meet the six teammates who plan, design, build, and validate your apps.

agents.responsibilities: Keeps Web Squad delivery moving — triaging issues, delegating work, reviewing PRs, and unblocking the team. Coordinates the pipeline from idea to QA sign-off.
agents.greeting: I'm Team Lead. I keep Web Squad delivery moving — triaging issues, delegating work, reviewing PRs, and unblocking the team. When you kick off a feature, I make sure scope is clear, design is ready, and the right engineer picks it up. You can count on me to coordinate the pipeline from idea to QA sign-off.

agents.responsibilities: Turns fuzzy ideas into clear requirements — user stories, acceptance criteria, and scoped handoffs engineering and QA can execute. Surfaces ambiguity early so the team does not rebuild later.
agents.greeting: I'm Business Analyst. I turn fuzzy ideas into clear requirements — user stories, acceptance criteria, and scoped handoffs engineering and QA can execute. If something is ambiguous, I ask the questions early so we don't rebuild later. I help you get from "we need a welcome screen" to testable, agreed-upon scope.

agents.responsibilities: Translates requirements into flows, layouts, and visual direction engineers can implement. Defines interaction states and keeps accessibility in mind from the start.
agents.greeting: I'm UX / UI Designer. I translate requirements into flows, layouts, and visual direction your engineers can implement. I define states — default, hover, focus, loading, empty, error — and keep accessibility in mind from the start. My job is to make the experience feel intentional before a single production component ships.

agents.responsibilities: Builds the React SPA — components, routing, client-side behavior, and polished UI that matches the design spec. Coordinates with Backend Engineer when data contracts change.
agents.greeting: I'm Frontend Engineer. I build the React SPA — components, routing, client-side behavior, and polished UI that matches the design spec. I work closely with Backend Engineer when data contracts change, and I care about responsive layout, keyboard access, and clean handoff for QA.

agents.responsibilities: Owns the Node.js API — endpoints, server logic, and the data layer that feeds the frontend. Designs stable contracts and documents how to run services locally.
agents.greeting: I'm Backend Engineer. I own the Node.js API — endpoints, server logic, and the data layer that feeds your frontend. For this app, that means serving agent metadata and health checks; on larger features, I design stable contracts and document how to run things locally.

agents.responsibilities: Validates shipped work against acceptance criteria — golden paths, edge cases, and error states — with clear pass/fail evidence before a feature is called done.
agents.greeting: I'm QA Engineer. I validate what we ship against acceptance criteria — golden paths, edge cases, and error states — with clear pass/fail evidence. I don't guess; I reproduce, document, and hand results back to the team. Before we call a feature done, I make sure it actually works the way we agreed.
